name: Ali Asghar (Abdullah al-Radhi)
title: Bab al-Hawa'ij
shortDescription: The six-month-old infant of Imam Hussain (AS) who was martyred with thirsty lips by a three-pronged arrow in his father's arms.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn al-Hussain, known as 'Ali Asghar', is the youngest child of Imam Hussain (AS) and Rubab bint Imra' al-Qais. This nursing infant is the symbol of absolute innocence and oppression in the tragedy of Karbala. His presence in Karbala demonstrated that Imam Hussain (AS) withheld not even the most beloved and defenseless members of his family to revive God's religion and awaken the Islamic Ummah.

In the days leading up to Ashura, when Yazid's army blocked the water supply to the Imam's caravan, severe thirst overcame the tents. The mother's milk had dried up, and the six-month-old infant was restless in his cradle from extreme thirst. The situation was so painful that it gripped the hearts of everyone in the camp, and the women could not soothe the child.

According to the documented report of the book Al-Irshad written by Sheikh Mufid, on the day of Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) returned from the battlefield to the tents, he said: 'Bring me my child so I may bid him farewell'. The Imam took the nursing infant in his arms and kissed him to say a final goodbye to his young child.

Sheikh Mufid continues in Al-Irshad: While the thirsty and restless child was in his father's arms, Harmala ibn Kahil al-Asadi (one of the skilled archers of Umar ibn Sa'd's army) shot a poisoned arrow towards them. This cruel arrow struck the delicate throat of the infant exactly, slaughtering him in his father's arms.

Imam Hussain (AS), faced with an infinitely painful scene, placed his hands under the child's throat. When his holy hands were filled with blood, he threw that pure blood towards the sky and said: 'What makes this tragedy easy for me to bear is that it happens in the sight of God'. The martyrdom of Ali Asghar showed the peak of cruelty of Yazid's army to history, forever eternalizing this six-month-old infant as the 'document of innocence' of Aba Abdillah al-Hussain (AS).
quoteArabic: هَوَّنَ عَلَیَّ ما نَزَلَ بي أَنَّهُ بِعَیْنِ اللّهِ
quote: What makes this tragedy easy to bear is that it happens in the sight of God.
